02
About

Who I Am

Location

Seoul, South Korea

Availability

Open to Remote (EU)

Languages

Korean (Native) · English (Professional)

Focus

React · TypeScript · Legacy Migration

I'm a full-stack web developer with a particular expertise in legacy system analysis and modern React migration. My most significant project involved reverse-engineering an entire legacy website from its source code and rebuilding it as a production-grade SPA.

What sets me apart is my ability to read and understand existing codebases — not just write new ones. I approach legacy systems with curiosity rather than frustration, finding the business logic embedded in years of accumulated code and preserving it while modernizing the architecture.

Development Philosophy

01

Code Archaeology

I read legacy codebases the way historians read ancient texts — finding the original intent beneath years of accumulated workarounds.

02

Incremental Modernization

I believe in surgical refactoring over big-bang rewrites. Each migration phase delivers working software while reducing technical debt.

03

Documentation-First

Every architectural decision I make is documented with rationale. Future developers (including myself) deserve to understand the 'why'.

03
Featured Case Study

Legacy Website → React SPA
Full Migration

A complete analysis and reconstruction of a legacy multi-page PHP/jQuery website into a modern, performant React Single-Page Application — without losing a single feature or business logic rule.

The Problem

The client had a 5-year-old PHP website with no version control, 47 separate HTML pages, and a jQuery codebase that had grown organically without architecture. Page load times averaged 8.4 seconds. Mobile experience was broken on 60% of devices.

No component reusability — every page duplicated navigation
jQuery version conflicts causing silent failures
No build process — all assets served unminified
Zero test coverage across 18,000+ lines of code
Business logic mixed with presentation in PHP templates

Migration Process — 14 Weeks Total

Phase 01

Legacy Code Archaeology

3 weeks
  • →Mapped all 47 HTML pages and their interdependencies
  • →Identified 12 distinct jQuery plugins with overlapping functionality
  • →Documented all server-side data endpoints (PHP/MySQL)
  • →Catalogued 3,200+ lines of inline CSS across templates
  • →Reverse-engineered business logic from spaghetti code

Outcome

Complete architectural blueprint of the legacy system

Tools Used

Chrome DevToolsRegex analysisDependency mappingGit blame

Measured Results

Page Load Time

Before

8.4s

→

After

2.0s

76% faster

Bundle Size

Before

2.4 MB

→

After

380 KB

84% smaller

Lighthouse Score

Before

42

→

After

94

+52 points

Code Lines

Before

18,400

→

After

6,200

66% reduced

Key Technical Decisions & Rationale

01

Decision

Zustand over Redux

The legacy app had minimal global state. Redux's boilerplate would have added complexity without benefit. Zustand provided the same patterns with 80% less code.

Trade-off Acknowledged

Less ecosystem tooling, but sufficient for project scale.

02

Decision

Vite over CRA

Legacy build process took 4+ minutes. Vite's ESM-based dev server reduced this to under 300ms HMR. Critical for developer productivity during the long migration.

Trade-off Acknowledged

Required custom plugin configuration for legacy asset handling.

03

Decision

Incremental Migration

Rather than a big-bang rewrite, I migrated one page group at a time. This allowed the legacy PHP backend to remain live while the React frontend was developed.

Trade-off Acknowledged

Required maintaining two codebases for 3 months, but eliminated downtime risk.

04
Projects

Selected Work

01

E-Commerce SPA Migration

Legacy Migration

✦ SEO-safe migration with zero ranking loss

2024

Problem

A 7-year-old WooCommerce site with 3-second TTFB and poor mobile conversion rates needed modernization without losing SEO rankings.

Solution

Analyzed the existing PHP templates and WooCommerce hooks, then rebuilt the frontend as a React SPA with Next.js for SSR/SSG to preserve SEO.

Result

TTFB reduced from 3.2s to 0.4s. Mobile conversion rate +34%.

ReactNext.jsTypeScriptTailwind CSSWooCommerce REST API
02

Internal Dashboard Rebuild

Greenfield + Migration

✦ 10,000+ rows rendered in 180ms

2023

Problem

A logistics company's internal tool was built with jQuery and Bootstrap 3. Data tables were slow with 10,000+ rows and no filtering.

Solution

Rebuilt as React SPA with TanStack Table for virtualized rendering, React Query for server state, and a custom filter system.

Result

Table render time: 4.8s → 180ms. User satisfaction score +62%.

React 18TanStack TableReact QueryZustandRecharts
03

Multi-Language Corporate Site

New Development

✦ GDPR-compliant, 4-language SPA

2024

Problem

A mid-size European manufacturer needed a new corporate website supporting 4 languages with CMS integration and GDPR compliance.

Solution

Built with React + i18next for internationalization, Contentful as headless CMS, and implemented cookie consent management per GDPR.

Result

Launched in 6 weeks. 4 languages live. GDPR audit passed.

Reacti18nextContentfulViteFramer Motion
04

Real Estate Listing Platform

Full-Stack SPA

✦ €800/month cost savings achieved

2023

Problem

A real estate agency was using a third-party platform that charged per listing. They needed an owned solution with map integration.

Solution

Built a full-stack SPA with React frontend, Node.js/Express API, PostgreSQL database, and Google Maps integration for property visualization.

Result

Saved €800/month in platform fees. 200+ listings managed.

ReactNode.jsExpressPostgreSQLGoogle Maps API

jQuery → React

Code Archaeology

Performance Audit

Note on Migration Skills: My ability to read and understand legacy PHP, jQuery, and unstructured HTML/CSS is a deliberate skill, not a relic. Understanding old code is the prerequisite for replacing it correctly. I've read codebases with no documentation, no version control, and no original developers available.
